title: Go bindings tutorial is out of date
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

The documentation in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/docs/tutorials/podman-go-bindings.md is out of date and refers to the old repository libpod. Please update it, I personally can't make sense of the new structure, what I need and what I don't need.

It would also be nice if the go package documentation would contain an introduction into how the go bindings fit together. As of now, there is only a per-function description of a single (unstructured) package here: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

@lsm5 would you update the tutorial with the latest 3.0 bindings? @baude FYI

I think we have some wires crossed.  The reporter is correct that the tutorial is out of date.  There is an updated how-to on the bindings right here -> https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/master/pkg/bindings.

However, the second link provided by @mqus are API calls "in" libpod.  It should be clarified that when we talk about bindings, we are referring to go-bindings that wrap the RESTFul API calls not the internal libpod calls.

That said, there have been a series of asks lately for more concise internal APIs for libpod. We are still debating the upside to doing that.

Thank you very much and sorry, I'm still a newbie when it comes to podman and the container ecosystem as a whole. I got to the linked godoc by following the godoc badge at the top of the main readme, so this must have misled me. 

Can I also use libpod to do effectively the same thing as the go bindings, but more directly, without having to set up a socket and daemon? Or are these APIs too raw and intended to be used internally?

Libpod can definitely do that, but the APIs it offers are not presently considered stable (meaning we reserve the right to change the public interface without a major version bump; that won't happen with the REST API). Practically speaking, though, the bigger issue is a lack of documentation - we have docs around the REST API, but almost none exist for Libpod.

I also really want to go through and do a proper cleanup of the public interface in Libpod at some point - there's a lot of implementation details leaking out that should not be in the API.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
